sources.label: adv-discover spec (rq-disc08 P25 Related-Pattern Scan, rq-disc-tax1/tax2 Ambiguity)
sources.summary: Discovery already owns a P25 related-pattern scan (Phase 1.7) and an 11-category AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS with B=Boundaries category + CRITICAL/2xHIGH halt-and-handoff trigger to /adv-clarify. Both are structural insertion points; no chokepoint concept exists yet.

sources.label: ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md Ambiguity Taxonomy (lines 599-652)
sources.summary: Canonical B=Boundaries category ('what is explicitly in/out of scope; edge cases', Required). CRITICAL=structurally-missing rubric. A false sole-chokepoint claim is a Boundaries CRITICAL: in-scope surface declared without proving sole entry point.

sources.label: docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md
sources.summary: P25 scan step already mandates lgrep semantic/symbol search for similar patterns; output-section schema + completeness heuristics define durable enforcement seams. Chokepoint verification can extend P25 step rather than add a parallel mechanism.

sources.label: Local grep for 'chokepoint|single entry point|single point of change' across *.md
sources.summary: Zero matches in any spec/command/checklist/instruction; only one incidental usage in an archived design doc. Confirms no current canonical rule under these terms — greenfield trigger naming.

sources.label: proposal.md Constraints + Context (coordination note)
sources.summary: Proposal already flags coordination with active change tightenAdvScopeDiscipline (prep/review scope bounding) and instructs discovery to confirm no duplicate spec delta. Also reports prior semantic lgrep timeouts — rerun with fresh index/symbol fallback during design.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The cleanest, boring-est leverage point is to SPECIALIZE existing discovery mechanisms rather than add a new phase. Two strong reuse seams: (1) the AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS B=Boundaries category already triggers a structural halt-and-handoff on CRITICAL findings — a false 'this is the chokepoint' claim is precisely a Boundaries CRITICAL (in-scope surface asserted without proof of sole entry point), so the trigger plumbing, severity rubric, and /adv-clarify handoff already exist and would be reused for free; (2) the P25 Related-Pattern Scan (Phase 1.7) already mandates lgrep semantic+symbol search and an output section — chokepoint surface-area scan is the same scan intent ('find all code paths performing operation X'), so it can be framed as a conditional specialization of P25 triggered by chokepoint/single-entry-point language, avoiding a redundant scan obligation. Both keep correctness structural (P33) via spec-law deltas + checklist output-schema rows + asset/command-consistency tests, which is the project's established enforcement pattern (document-only checklist backed by adv-discover spec rq-* + asset tests). Adding a brand-new standalone 'chokepoint phase' would be the clever-but-novel path and should be avoided.

validation.notes: No contract/security/release blockers. Evidence access fully succeeded except adv_change_list timed out (known Temporal symptom; proposal already records prior lgrep timeouts). Coordination with tightenAdvScopeDiscipline must be verified at design to avoid duplicate spec deltas — flagged as follow-up, not a blocker.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt the trigger-scoped specialization approach: (a) define a precise trigger condition (chokepoint / sole entry point / single point of change / shared cross-cutting control surface claim) so narrow changes are NOT forced into repo-wide scans (honors proposal Must-Not); (b) extend the P25 Related-Pattern Scan step to require a comprehensive target-operation surface scan (lgrep_search_semantic + lgrep_search_symbols) when the trigger fires, with recorded methodology (terms searched, surfaces found, surfaces excluded + rationale, scope disposition); (c) wire the over-claim outcome into the existing B=Boundaries AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS as a CRITICAL finding so the existing halt-and-handoff-to-/adv-clarify path is reused; (d) make it durable via adv-discover spec rq-* delta + discover-checklist output-section row + an asset/command-consistency test, not prose alone. Confirm non-duplication with tightenAdvScopeDiscipline before minting spec deltas.
follow_ups: Design must verify no overlapping/duplicate spec delta with active change tightenAdvScopeDiscipline (prep/review scope bounding) per proposal Constraints.
Rerun lgrep semantic/symbol search with fresh index during design; proposal records two prior semantic-search timeouts.
Design to decide durable home: extend adv-discover spec (recommended) vs advance-workflow spec vs both; recommend adv-discover to keep locality (P04).
Define exact trigger vocabulary bounds and an explicit opt-out marker for narrow changes to satisfy the 'do not force repo-wide scans' Must-Not.

subagent_reports.topic: Design leverage scout — discovery completeness verification

sources.label: plugin/src/validator/completeness.ts (whole file, 225 lines)
sources.summary: runCompletenessChecks operates on Change.deltas + Change.tasks (scenarios, IDs, TDD evidence, contract). It is NOT a discovery-output-text validator. No seam to assert problem-completeness/solution-scope text. CONTEXT Q1 answer: no existing completeness validator the spec/asset test can reuse for discovery prose.

sources.label: docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md ('All 10 protocol steps')
sources.summary: Hardcoded step count 'All 10 protocol steps' appears at :24 and again at :139 (Completeness Heuristics). Adding a Completeness Verification protocol step forces a lockstep edit of BOTH to 11 (DDC4-style drift). Output Section Schema table (:60-78) is the single derivation point for a Completeness Verification row.

sources.label: plugin/src grep for protocol-step counts in *.test.ts
sources.summary: No asset/spec test hardcodes the discovery protocol-step count. CONTEXT Q2 answer: drift risk is checklist-prose-only (2 lines), not test-enforced — low blast radius, but still must be updated in lockstep or the checklist self-contradicts.

sources.label: plugin/src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts COMMAND_BASELINES + token-budgets.json
sources.summary: adv-discover.md baseline = 358 lines; the warn-only test uses threshold = ceil(baseline*1.1)=394. A new Phase 1.8 block must stay under +36 lines or bump the baseline. Advisory (always passes) — not a hard gate, but surfaces noise.

sources.label: adv-discover spec rq-disc-tax2 (B-CRITICAL halt) + rq-disc08 (P25)
sources.summary: rq-disc-tax2.1 already fires halt+handoff-to-/adv-clarify on a single CRITICAL B(Boundaries) finding. Checklist Trigger Evaluation table (:110-115) wires this. Sole-entry block reuses this existing path verbatim (design KD2 confirmed) — zero new halt machinery. CONTEXT Q4 answer: block is agent-emitted B-CRITICAL finding routed through existing trigger eval; no helper function exists or is needed.

sources.label: discover-checklist Output Section Schema table
sources.summary: Command Phase prose and checklist each independently list output sections; there is NO shared constant. CONTEXT Q3 answer: no single 'discovery output section schema' constant — command + checklist + spec must each gain the Completeness Verification reference; they do not auto-propagate.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Five leverage points, all favoring the boring/reuse path the draft design already chose. (1) The strongest simplification is NEGATIVE: completeness.ts is the wrong reuse target — it validates delta/task structure, not discovery prose — so the design must NOT wire a code validator; spec+command+checklist+asset-anchor text enforcement (KD5) is correct and is the only realistic structural home given OOS5 (no target-codebase validator) and C6 (no Temporal surface). (2) The sole-entry block correctly reuses rq-disc-tax2 B-CRITICAL halt (KD2) — confirmed it exists and is checklist-wired; no new helper, agent emits the finding manually and the existing trigger-eval table routes it. (3) Real drift hazard: 'All 10 protocol steps' is hardcoded at two checklist lines (:24, :139) and becomes 11 — must be edited in lockstep, but NO test enforces the count, so blast radius is just self-consistency of one file (low risk, easy to miss). (4) The Output Section Schema table is the single best insertion point: add ONE 'Completeness Verification' row there and reference it from command Phase 1.8 + spec rq-disc; there is no shared constant so all three surfaces need the reference but the checklist table is the canonical schema. (5) Parallelism: spec.json + docs/specs mirror + command + checklist can all be edited in ONE engineer task — no test sequences them, asset tests assert presence of tokens (spec IDs, section headers, phase names) independently, so a single atomic commit is stable. Token budget for adv-discover.md (+36 line warn threshold) is the only soft constraint.

validation.notes: No contract/security/release blockers. All design KDs are evidence-supported. The only correction to surface: do not attempt to extend completeness.ts (validator operates on deltas/tasks, not discovery output) — confirm the design's KD5 keeps enforcement at spec/command/checklist/asset-anchor text only.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed with the draft design unchanged on architecture. Apply two boring leverage adjustments at planning: (A) Add the 'All 10 → 11 protocol steps' edit to BOTH discover-checklist.md:24 and :139 as an explicit task line item (DDC4-prevention) since no test will catch the miss. (B) Make spec.json + docs/specs/adv-discover.md mirror + .opencode/command/adv-discover.md + discover-checklist.md a SINGLE atomic engineer task (no sequencing needed — asset tests assert token presence independently), with the Output Section Schema 'Completeness Verification' row as the canonical anchor referenced by command Phase 1.8. Explicitly do NOT touch plugin/src/validator/completeness.ts — it is not the discovery-completeness seam. Keep new Phase 1.8 prose under +36 lines to stay below the adv-discover.md token warn threshold (baseline 358 → 394).
follow_ups: Planning: add explicit task line to update 'All 10 protocol steps' → 11 at discover-checklist.md:24 AND :139 (untested drift, easy to miss).
Planning: single atomic engineer task for spec+docs-mirror+command+checklist (no sequencing required; asset tests are presence-based and independent).
Design/engineer note: completeness.ts is NOT reusable for discovery prose enforcement — keep KD5 text-anchor approach; do not wire a code validator.
Keep new Phase 1.8 command prose under ~36 added lines or bump token-budgets.json adv-discover.md baseline (358) to avoid the warn-only token-budget noise.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation — Add Discovery Completeness Verification

sources.label: adv-discover spec rq-disc08 (P25 Related-Pattern Scan)
sources.summary: P25 scan = 'detect similar bugs or gaps elsewhere'. Distinct question from completeness ('did we find the whole problem'). Confirms KD4 separation is spec-grounded, not redundant.

sources.label: adv-discover spec rq-disc-tax1/tax2 (AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS + mandatory /adv-clarify trigger)
sources.summary: B=Boundaries is a REQUIRED v1 category; a CRITICAL B finding halts discovery and hands off to /adv-clarify with adv_gate_complete NOT called. Confirms KD2 reuse path exists and is exactly the plumbing the design claims (zero new halt code).

sources.label: adv-discover spec requirement ID space
sources.locator: adv_spec action:show capability:adv-discover (21/21 requirements, highest = rq-disc12 + rq-disc-tax3 + rq-sc01)
sources.summary: No existing rq-disc13/14/15. Proposed IDs do not collide. Spec version 1.3.0 — design's version-bump step is correct.

sources.label: agreement.md constraints C1-C6 + avoidances DONT1-DONT6
sources.summary: Design KDs map 1:1 to constraints: KD1↔C1/DONT3, KD3↔C3, KD5↔C2, KD7↔C6, secondary-disposition↔AC4/DONT4. No avoidance violated.

sources.label: P33 structural-before-heuristic (global rules.yaml)
sources.summary: Requires machine-checkable correctness over prose. Design honors via spec+command+checklist+asset-test anchors, but R3 concedes check DEPTH/QUALITY remains agent-honor — partial structural coverage, accepted per OOS5.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is correct and spec-law compliant across all four dimensions. CORRECTNESS: the three-part discipline (problem-completeness, solution-scope, sole-entry blocking) maps directly to AC1/AC2/AC3; secondary-surface disposition maps to AC4; durable anchors to AC5; depth-scaling to AC6. The blocking case correctly reuses the existing rq-disc-tax2.1 B-CRITICAL→halt→/adv-clarify path (verified: B is a required ambiguity category, CRITICAL halts, gate not completed), so no new halt machinery is invented — KD2 is sound. SIMPLICITY: the boring/locally-correct choice. It extends the phase that already owns discovery rigor (sibling to rq-disc08 P25) and reuses battle-tested ambiguity plumbing; the standalone-analyzer alternative is correctly ruled OOS. No materially simpler approach meets AC3 (a real block) AND AC5 (durable structural anchors) — pure prose was explicitly rejected as the current weakness (R3/KD5). SPEC-LAW COMPLIANCE: no contradiction with existing adv-discover requirements; rq-disc13/14/15 IDs are free; spec version bump planned; KD3 keeps advance-workflow untouched per C3. KEY ALTERNATIVES: the one significant alternative (reuse completeness.ts validator) was investigated and correctly rejected in KD5 — that validator operates on deltas/tasks not discovery prose. One residual: a structural step-count invariant (DDC4) is enforced only by asset-test token presence, not by a computed check — minor brittleness, acceptable.

validation.notes: VALIDATED. All 4 dimensions pass with spec evidence. Two non-blocking cautions: (1) P33 partial coverage — check depth/quality stays agent-honor (design discloses this in R3, bounded by OOS5 and the KD2 blocking backstop for the highest-stakes case; acceptable). (2) DDC4 step-count invariant is anchored by asset-test token presence rather than a derived count assertion — recommend the asset test assert the SAME literal step count in both command and checklist so they cannot drift independently.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to planning. The design solves all six ACs with spec-grounded mechanics and chooses the simplest durable approach (extend Phase 1.7 sibling + reuse rq-disc-tax2 halt). No CONFLICT with existing adv-discover law; rq-disc13/14/15 are collision-free and advance-workflow is correctly left untouched (C3). Address one CAUTION in prep: make the DDC4 step-count invariant cross-checked by the asset test (assert command and checklist reference the identical protocol step count, e.g. both say 9/11) so they cannot silently diverge — this converts the weakest structural anchor from presence-only to drift-resistant, strengthening the P33 posture without new scope.
follow_ups: adv_spec FTS search returned empty for completeness/chokepoint/boundary queries; validation relied on the full paginated spec.show (21/21 requirements) instead — note possible search-index staleness, not a design issue.
Prep: add a derived step-count cross-check in the asset test (DDC4) rather than independent presence tokens.
Confirm at prep that ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md pointer edit does not collide with tightenAdvScopeDiscipline's shared-surface edits (C4).

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Phase 1.8 command content directly implements problem-completeness, solution-scope, sole-entry blocking, secondary-surface disposition, and scan-depth scaling.
nonblocking_findings.why: Command is the primary owned surface for always-on discovery behavior and matches rq-disc13/rq-disc14 plus AC1-AC4/AC6 after review.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: rq-disc15.2 records co-presence rather than numeric step-count equality.
nonblocking_findings.why: Command and checklist intentionally use different step granularities; co-presence is a legitimate design refinement and avoids a false invariant.

changes_made.summary: Strengthened the discovery completeness asset test to read `docs/specs/adv-discover.md` and assert rq-disc13/rq-disc14/rq-disc15 co-presence in both canonical spec JSON and docs mirror. This fixes AC5/rq-disc15 mismatch where the spec promised docs-mirror asset coverage but the test only inspected spec JSON, command, and checklist.

wisdom_candidates.content: When command/checklist protocol surfaces intentionally use different granularities, asset tests should enforce semantic co-presence across durable surfaces rather than numeric step-count equality; if the spec text says docs mirror is covered, the test must actually read the docs mirror.

First targeted test invocation used repo-root paths and exited 1 with no files found; rerun with plugin-relative paths passed 67/67 tests. Reviewed command/spec/docs/checklist/test surfaces for AC1-AC6, C3/C6, DONT/OOS; no remaining blockers or issues.

required_main_agent_actions: If a contract review matrix is missing, orchestrator should persist one with adv_contract_review_matrix_set; subagent did not mutate ADV orchestration state.
Do not revisit numeric protocol step-count equality for rq-disc15.2; co-presence is the reviewed invariant because command and checklist use different granularities.

No CONFLICT, no contract compromise. advance-workflow untouched (C3). No new Temporal surface (C6).

Contract review matrix 27/27 pass, 0 failing. 67/67 asset tests pass. pnpm run check exit 0. Executive summary persisted. No scope drift. Design refinement rq-disc15.2 (co-presence) recorded in spec + docs.

items.text: Discovery stops treating "found one symptom/path" as "found the full problem / full solution" without explicit completeness evidence.
Sole-path / chokepoint mistakes surface before design/prep, not at acceptance.
Design/review get an auditable record: searched surfaces, found surfaces, excluded surfaces, final scope disposition.
Every discovery runs an always-on **problem-completeness check**: was the full problem identified, not only an observed symptom/path?
Every discovery runs an always-on **solution-scope check**: is the full intended solution scoped, not only one implementation piece?
When discovery relies on a sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claim, discovery **blocks** until the claim is verified or downgraded.
Any secondary surfaces found during completeness verification must be classified **in scope / out of scope with rationale / unresolved user scope question** before agreement.
The obligation is durable in ADV law/docs/tests: `adv-discover` spec + docs mirror + command/checklist surface + asset-test anchor.
The completeness **check** always runs; the codebase surface-scan **depth** scales to what the completeness question demands, preserving proportionality for narrow changes.
Always-on completeness check; no trigger gate. Scan depth scales to the question; narrow changes are not forced into broad repository-wide scans.
Correctness stays structural: spec law + command/checklist output shape + asset-test anchors own it; heuristics assist only (P33).
Primary home: `adv-discover` spec/checklist/command. Touch `advance-workflow` only if design finds cross-gate law necessary.
Coordinate with `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` on any shared `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `advance-workflow` edits.
Preserve discovery as the phase that firms design-independent objectives, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries.
Structural checks are tool-layer/spec/checklist/asset anchors only — no new Temporal workflow signal, query, or `defineUpdate` surface.
Don't treat first-found call sites or first-observed symptoms as proof of full problem/solution scope.
Don't rely on heuristic prose alone where a structural spec/checklist/test anchor can own correctness.
Don't gate the completeness *check* behind a trigger condition — the check is always-on; only scan *depth* scales.
Don't silently defer secondary surfaces as "future work" without explicit scope rationale.
Don't force narrow changes into broad repository-wide scans when the completeness question does not demand it.
Don't replace discovery's P25 related-pattern scan wholesale — extend/specialize it.
Editing the pokeedge project or retroactively fixing `addResidentialProxyExternal`.
Designing a language-specific static analyzer for every cross-cutting concern.
Replacing P25 related-pattern scan wholesale.
Changing the seven-gate lifecycle or moving discovery responsibilities into design/review.
Building a target-codebase-aware sole-entry validator (structural enforcement limited to spec + command + checklist + asset anchors).
Adding a new Temporal workflow signal/query/`defineUpdate` surface.

rq-disc14.2 + Phase 1.8 sole-entry blocking (B-CRITICAL via rq-disc-tax2) surfaces unverified chokepoint claims before design/prep.
Phase 1.8 target-operation surface scan evidence shape (searched terms/symbols, found/excluded surfaces, disposition) gives design/review an auditable record.

workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts command contains 'solution-scope'; Phase 1.8 solution-scope check present.
workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts B-CRITICAL/Boundaries CRITICAL; rq-disc14.2 + Phase 1.8 sole-entry blocking reuse rq-disc-tax2 halt.
Phase 1.8 secondary-surface disposition (in/out/unsolved) in command + checklist edge-case rows; discover-checklist.md requires classification before agreement.

rq-disc13/14/15 in spec.json + docs mirror; Phase 1.8 in command + checklist; ADV_INSTRUCTIONS pointer; asset anchors in 2 test files. workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts spec IDs + command + checklist + docs mirror co-presence.
workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts 'scan depth scales'; Phase 1.8 scan-depth-scaling section + rq-disc13.2.
Phase 1.8 is always-on (not trigger-gated); scan-depth-scaling section in adv-discover.md + checklist edge row.
Spec law (rq-disc13/14/15) + command + checklist + asset-test anchors own the obligation; no heuristic-only prose.
Primary home adv-discover; advance-workflow spec untouched (only stale test assertion campsite-fixed 1.17→1.18).
ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md edit is a one-line pointer only (single-source-per-surface); sibling change touches prep/review.
Discovery still firms design-independent objectives/AC; completeness check does not move criteria firming out of discovery.
No new Temporal signal/query/defineUpdate; enforcement is spec/command/checklist/test only. workflow-bundle-boundary untouched.
Phase 1.8 explicitly requires verifying sole-entry claims; rq-disc14.2 blocks unverified claims.
Obligation owned by spec rq-* + asset tests, not prose alone.
Phase 1.8 runs always-on; only scan depth is conditional.
Phase 1.8 + checklist forbid silently deferring secondary surfaces; require explicit disposition.
Scan-depth-scaling section + rq-disc13.2 record lightweight rationale for narrow changes; no forced broad scan.
Phase 1.8 is separate from P25 (rq-disc08); documented as complementary, not merged.
pokeedge not edited; only ADV repo surfaces touched.
No language-specific static analyzer built.
P25 (rq-disc08) preserved; Phase 1.8 is additive.
Seven-gate lifecycle unchanged; discovery still owns agreement.
No target-codebase-aware validator; enforcement is spec/command/checklist/test anchors only.
No new Temporal surface added.

Every discovery runs an always-on **solution-scope check**: is the full intended solution scoped, not only one implementation piece?

documents.proposal:
# Add Discovery Completeness Verification

> **Change ID note:** The immutable change ID remains `addChokepointVerification` (workflow/branch/directory key). The scope was broadened at discovery from "chokepoint verification" to always-on discovery completeness verification (problem + solution scope), with chokepoint/sole-entry as one hard-block instance. Artifacts carry the broadened framing.

## Why

During discovery, ADV can treat "found one symptom/path" as "found the full problem," and "scoped one implementation piece" as "scoped the full solution," without verifying completeness.

Observed failure: in `addResidentialProxyExternal`, discovery identified `_download_image` in `firecrawl_handler.py` as the chokepoint for anonymous image fetches. A second path, `ImageDownloadClient.download()` in `clients/r2/r2_client.py`, served multiple handlers and was missed. Result: agreement said PikaQian image CDN would be proxied, but implementation only touched `_download_image`; PikaQian images flowed through untouched code.

Root cause: discovery searched narrow call sites for one function instead of asking whether the full problem and full solution scope were captured.

Desired outcome: discovery runs an always-on completeness discipline — did we find the full problem (not just a symptom), and is the full intended solution scoped (not just one piece) — with structural evidence and explicit disposition.

## What Changes

- Add an **always-on problem-completeness check** to discovery: was the full problem identified, not only an observed symptom/path?
- Add an **always-on solution-scope check**: is the full intended solution scoped, not only one implementation piece?
- When discovery relies on a sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claim, discovery **blocks** until the claim is verified or downgraded.
- Require any **secondary surfaces** found during completeness verification to be explicitly routed: in scope, out of scope with rationale, or unresolved user scope question.
- Require **search-methodology evidence** (searched terms/symbols, found surfaces, excluded surfaces, disposition) carried forward so design/review can audit completeness.
- Make the obligation **durable**: `adv-discover` spec law + docs mirror + command/checklist output shape + asset-test anchors.

## User Outcomes

- Users can trust that discovery asked "did we find the whole problem and scope the whole solution," not just the first surface found.
- Cross-cutting changes are less likely to miss parallel code paths that perform the same user-relevant operation.
- Design, prep, and review receive auditable completeness evidence instead of validating only the implemented path.
- When secondary surfaces exist, users see the scope decision before implementation planning rather than after a missed acceptance outcome.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Discovery workflow/spec behavior for always-on problem- and solution-completeness checks.
- Discovery checklist and command instructions that govern completeness evidence and surface disposition.
- Spec-law deltas for the affected ADV discovery capability (primary home `adv-discover`).
- Tests or asset checks needed to keep the new discovery obligation durable.
- Documentation of how discovery records searched terms, scanned symbols/semantics, found surfaces, excluded surfaces, and scope disposition.

### Out of Scope

- Editing the pokeedge project or retroactively fixing `addResidentialProxyExternal`.
- Designing a language-specific static analyzer for every possible cross-cutting concern.
- Replacing discovery's existing P25 related-pattern scan wholesale (extend, don't replace).
- Changing the seven-gate lifecycle or moving discovery responsibilities into design/review.
- Building a target-codebase-aware sole-entry validator (out of scope per proposal; structural enforcement limited to spec + command + checklist + asset anchors).

### Must Not

- Must not treat first-found call sites or first-observed symptoms as proof of full problem/solution scope.
- Must not rely on heuristic prose alone where a structural spec/checklist/test anchor can own correctness.
- Must not gate the completeness *check* behind a trigger condition — the check is always-on; only scan *depth* scales to the question.
- Must not silently defer secondary surfaces as "future work" without explicit scope rationale.
- Must not force narrow changes into broad repository-wide scans when the completeness question does not demand it.

## Affected Code

- `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md` — discovery phase contract and required output sections.
- `docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md` — discovery checklist, P25 related-pattern scan, completeness heuristics.
- `.adv/specs/adv-discover/spec.json` and `docs/specs/adv-discover.md` — durable law for discovery rigor (primary home).
- `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` and `docs/specs/advance-workflow.md` — only if design finds cross-gate law necessary.
- `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` — only if a concise canonical invariant is warranted.
- Asset or spec tests covering command/spec/checklist consistency (e.g., `plugin/src/workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts`, `plugin/src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts`).

## Related Repositories

- Current repo: `advance`.
- Primary affected capability: `advance-workflow`; discovery-specific spec `adv-discover` is the primary home.
- The motivating miss occurred in the separate pokeedge project, but this change does not edit pokeedge. It uses that incident as evidence for ADV process behavior.
- No product-linked multi-repo scope detected in `project.json`.

## Constraints

- **Always-on completeness check; no trigger gate.** Scan depth scales to the question; narrow changes are not forced into broad repository-wide scans.
- Correctness stays structural: spec law + command/checklist output shape + asset-test anchors own it; heuristics assist only.
- Primary home: `adv-discover` spec/checklist/command. Touch `advance-workflow` only if design finds cross-gate law necessary.
- Coordinate with active change `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` on any shared `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `advance-workflow` edits.
- Preserve discovery as the phase that firms design-independent objectives, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries.

## Impact

- Discovery becomes more resistant to "found one path = found the whole problem" assumptions.
- Design and review gain auditable evidence for whether the full problem and full solution scope were captured.
- Every discovery runs the completeness check; only those whose question demands it run broad surface scans, so narrow changes stay proportional.
- The new requirement adds discovery output, so design should keep evidence concise and structured.

## Context

Quick checks completed during proposal:

- `adv-discover` owns current-state evidence, objectives, acceptance criteria, and agreement output.
- `docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md` already requires P25 related-pattern scan and codebase searches, but does not mention problem/solution completeness or chokepoint verification.
- Exact local searches for `chokepoint`, `single entry point`, `sole entry`, `single point of change`, `surface scan`, `secondary surface`, and `search methodology` returned no matching current rule.
- Existing discovery spec `adv-discover` includes conflict scan, related-pattern scan, LBP check, ambiguity analysis, and acceptance-criteria checkpoint requirements; discovery extends that law rather than creating an unrelated mechanism.

## Knowledge Gap Analysis

### Codebase unknowns

- Exact best insertion point resolved at discovery: extend `adv-discover` spec/checklist/command + asset anchors.
- Existing tests identified: `workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts` and `adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts` are suitable anchor homes.

### Ecosystem unknowns

- No external ecosystem dependency; internal workflow/spec-law change. "No external alternatives apply."

### Domain unknowns

- Trigger vocabulary resolved by user decision: always-on check (no trigger); scan depth scales to the question.

### Integration unknowns

- `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` is complementary (prep/review task→AC bounding vs discovery problem→solution scope); coordinate shared instruction/spec edits at design.
- P25 related-pattern scan is extended/specialized, not replaced.

## Discovery Agenda

- All items resolved at discovery or deferred to design with rationale (see Discovery Findings and agreement).

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Status | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Skill Discovery | PASS | Loaded `adv-instructions-audit` and `adv-audit`; no pending agent-created skill; no new skill needed. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | `temp/*.md` absent; `docs/*-prep.md` packs inspected and not relevant; archived-list read timed out twice (degraded). |
| Conflict & Related-Work Scan | PASS | `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` related but complementary; own-change `NO_TASKS`/`NO_DELTAS` expected pre-prep; no chokepoint-specific agenda item. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Edge cases listed below. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | Design questions annotated with trust model, blast radius, alternatives. |
| Draft Spec Delta Shapes | PASS | Draft `rq-disc13`/`rq-disc14`/`rq-disc15` shapes below. |
| P25 Related-Pattern Scan | PASS | Delegated codebase scan found no existing chokepoint/sole-entry rule; P25 is nearest extension point. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Internal structural spec/checklist/asset-test route is LBP; no external alternatives apply. |
| Discovery Opportunity Scout | PASS | 5 candidates; 3 auto-adopt, 2 design/user-surface. |

### Skills Considered

| Skill | Match | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `adv-instructions-audit` | Strong — edits command/checklist/instruction surfaces needing anchor tests. | Loaded; apply trace-to-anchor in design. |
| `adv-audit` | Strong — adds spec law; avoid spec/code drift. | Loaded; use spec-parser/drift-scanner framing. |
| New `agent-*` skill | Not needed. | Not created. |

### Extends

- No relevant `temp/*.md` prior artifact.
- `docs/*-prep.md` packs target unrelated domains (adv_run_test, slop-scan, repo perf).
- New finding: discovery has no canonical completeness/chokepoint vocabulary and no surface-completeness rule.
- Archived change scan degraded (timeouts); proceeds with limitation recorded.

### Conflict Scan

- `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline`: related, complementary (prep/review task→AC bounding vs discovery problem→solution scope). Coordinate shared `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `advance-workflow` edits.
- `adv_change_validate`: passed; `NO_TASKS`/`NO_DELTAS` expected pre-prep.
- `adv_agenda_list`: no chokepoint-specific item.
- Archived scan: timed out twice; not a blocker.

### Current State

- `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md:175-185` Phase 1.7 P25 related-pattern scan; `:195-208` output sections lack a completeness/surface section.
- `docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md:5` states checklist enforcement is document-only; `:20` requires P25 scan.
- `docs/specs/adv-discover.md:291-310` and `.adv/specs/adv-discover/spec.json` define `rq-disc08` (P25).
- `plugin/src/workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts:111-121` suitable anchor home (reads command + checklist).
- `plugin/src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts:276-295` asserts `adv-discover` spec IDs.
- `plugin/src/delegation-matrix.test.ts:432-449` + `.adv/specs/delegation-defaults/spec.json:17-25` route discovery P25 as delegated wide scan.
- No current rule mentions `chokepoint`, `single entry point`, `sole entry`, or `single point of change`.

### Edge Cases

| Gap | Edge case / failure mode |
| --- | --- |
| False sole-chokepoint claim | Function is chokepoint for one provider but a parallel client serves others. |
| False sole-chokepoint claim | Operation exists behind a different abstraction name; narrow lookup misses it. |
| Completeness-check shallowness | Discovery says "searched codebase" without terms/symbols/results, leaving no auditable evidence. |
| Completeness-check shallowness | Secondary surfaces found but left as vague future work. |
| Scan-depth proportionality | Always-on check degenerates into broad repo scans on narrow changes. |
| Coordination | `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` edits shared instruction/spec surfaces; uncoordinated edits duplicate/conflict. |
| Tool degradation | lgrep semantic search or archived visibility times out; workflow needs fallback evidence. |

### Open Design Questions

| Question | Trust model | Blast radius if wrong | Alternatives / recommendation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Completeness-check evidence shape | Agent-only | Weak evidence leaves design/review unable to audit. | Recommended: searched terms/symbols, found/excluded surfaces, disposition, confidence. |
| Primary durable home | Agent-only | Wrong home scatters law, increases drift. | Recommended: `adv-discover` spec/checklist/command; `advance-workflow` only if cross-gate law needed. |
| P25 extension vs standalone phase | Agent-only | Standalone duplicates P25; buried extension missed. | Recommended: extend P25 with trigger-scoped completeness subsection + output row. |
| Asset-test scope | Agent-only | Prose-only drifts; over-testing brittle text slows iteration. | Recommended: anchor presence across spec, command, checklist, docs mirror, one asset test. |
| ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md invariant | Agent-only | Extra prose may bloat; absent invariant may be missed. | Deferred to design: decide if concise invariant warranted. |

### Draft Spec Deltas

- `rq-disc13` — **Always-On Problem Completeness Check**
  - Given: a discovery invocation.
  - When: discovery prepares findings for agreement.
  - Then: discovery records an explicit problem-completeness check answering whether the full problem was identified, not only an observed symptom/path, with rationale.
- `rq-disc14` — **Always-On Solution-Scope Completeness Check + Secondary Surface Disposition**
  - Given: discovery identifies the intended solution scope, including any sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claim.
  - When: discovery forms objectives/acceptance criteria.
  - Then: discovery records a solution-scope check answering whether the full intended solution is scoped; any secondary surfaces found are classified in scope / out of scope with rationale / unresolved user scope question; an unverified sole-entry claim blocks until verified or downgraded.
- `rq-disc15` — **Durable Completeness Verification Anchors**
  - Given: discovery completeness obligations are added.
  - When: implementation updates command/checklist/spec surfaces.
  - Then: asset tests assert the obligation is present in `adv-discover` command, checklist, canonical spec, and docs mirror so prose cannot drift silently.

### Related Pattern Scan

- No existing chokepoint/sole-entry/surface-scan rule found.
- Closest rule is P25 (`rq-disc08`): scans for similar bugs/gaps after a class is known; does not prove a claimed sole operational entry point is sole. Complementary, extended.
- Related completeness patterns: archive completeness validation, cross-repo routing coverage, spec scenario completeness — support structural evidence over prose.

### LBP Check

- Direction: extend existing discovery law and P25 routing; always-on check, depth-scaled scan.
- Rationale: locality of behavior keeps obligations near `rq-disc08`; structural correctness via spec law + output shape + asset anchors.
- External-Solution Check: skipped with rationale — internal workflow/spec-law correction; no external alternative is realistic.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

| Candidate | Route | Decision |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Reuse B=Boundaries ambiguity trigger for false sole-chokepoint claims | Auto-adopt | Adopted into AC (block unverified sole-entry). |
| Specialize P25 into trigger-scoped surface scan | Auto-adopt | Adopted into design direction. |
| Greenfield trigger vocabulary | User-surface | Resolved: always-on check (no trigger). |
| Durable spec + checklist + asset-test enforcement | Auto-adopt | Adopted into AC. |
| Coordinate with `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` | Design-gate check | Carried as constraint. |

### Discovery Question Resolutions (User Decisions)

1. **Trigger scope** → "Don't rely on a trigger; persistent always-on check that the FULL problem was found (not just a symptom) and the FULL solution is scoped (not just a piece)." Reframed AC1/AC2 as always-on; AC6 captures depth-scaled scan.
2. **Strictness** → "Block until verified." Adopted as AC3.
3. **Secondary paths** → "Disposition required." Adopted as AC4.
4. **Title/scope** → Broadened from "Add chokepoint verification" to "Add discovery completeness verification." Chokepoint/sole-entry is one hard-block instance.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

M1  LOW  Missing Information  None remaining after user decisions.
  Evidence: `(resolved via Discovery Question Resolutions)`
  Reason: n/a.

Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C

Trigger evaluation: clean; proceed to agreement.

### Recommended Objectives

1. Add always-on problem-completeness and solution-scope checks to discovery.
2. Require surface-scan methodology evidence and explicit disposition of secondary surfaces.
3. Block unverified sole-chokepoint/single-entry claims until verified or downgraded.
4. Extend P25/discovery output shape, not replace it.
5. Make the obligation durable via `adv-discover` spec law + docs mirror + command/checklist + asset-test anchors.
6. Coordinate with `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` on shared instruction/workflow-spec surfaces.
documents.problemStatement:
# Problem Statement: Add chokepoint completeness verification to discovery

## Problem

During `addResidentialProxyExternal` (pokeedge project), the discovery phase identified `_download_image` in `firecrawl_handler.py` as "the chokepoint" for anonymous image fetches — the single point where proxy routing should be applied. It wasn't. It was **one of two** parallel download surfaces in the codebase.

A second surface, `ImageDownloadClient.download()` in `clients/r2/r2_client.py`, was used by 5 other handlers (poke52_handler, pikaqian_handler, image_download_worker, cn_image_rehost_service, _card_operations_service). None were identified during discovery. The design agreement explicitly said PikaQian image CDN would be proxied, but the implementation plan targeted `_download_image` only — PikaQian images actually flow through `ImageDownloadClient`, which was never touched.

## Root Cause

The discovery searched for "where does `_download_image` get called" (narrow call-site lookup) instead of "where does the codebase download images from external hosts" (comprehensive surface-area scan). The chokepoint was identified from one surface without verifying it was the sole entry point.

## Cascade

1. Discovery: identified `_download_image` as the chokepoint — no verification it was the sole download path
2. Researcher: confirmed call sites in `firecrawl_handler.py` — didn't ask "are there other download paths?"
3. Design: scoped to `_download_image` — assumed PikaQian images go through it (they don't)
4. Planning: 3 tasks, all in `firecrawl_handler.py`
5. Review: verified implemented paths — didn't audit for missed surfaces
6. Acceptance: contract matrix 20/20 — tested implemented scope, not intended scope

## What Needs to Change

When the discovery phase identifies a "chokepoint" or "single point of change" for a cross-cutting concern (proxy routing, auth, rate limiting, caching, etc.), it must:

1. **Verify the chokepoint is the SOLE entry point** — not just one of multiple
2. **Run a comprehensive surface scan** using semantic/symbol search (`lgrep_search_semantic` / `lgrep_search_symbols`) for ALL code paths that perform the target operation (e.g., "download image from external host", "fetch from anonymous URL")
3. **Explicitly document secondary surfaces** — any additional entry points found must be either scoped into the change or marked out-of-scope with explicit rationale
4. **Record the search methodology** — what was searched, what was found, what was excluded, so the design phase and reviewer can audit completeness

The fix is structural (P33): a deterministic verification step in the discovery workflow, not a heuristic assumption that the first surface found is the only one.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — addChokepointVerification (scope: Add Discovery Completeness Verification)

> Change ID `addChokepointVerification` is immutable (workflow/branch/directory key). Scope broadened at discovery to always-on discovery completeness verification; chokepoint/sole-entry is one hard-block instance.

## Objectives

1. Add always-on problem-completeness and solution-scope checks to discovery.
2. Require surface-scan methodology evidence and explicit disposition of secondary surfaces.
3. Block unverified sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claims until verified or downgraded.
4. Extend P25 / discovery output shape rather than replacing it or creating a broad always-on repository scan.
5. Make the obligation durable through `adv-discover` spec law, command/checklist updates, docs mirror, and asset-test anchors.
6. Coordinate with `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` when touching shared instruction/workflow-spec surfaces.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: Discovery stops treating "found one symptom/path" as "found the full problem / full solution" without explicit completeness evidence.
- SC2: Sole-path / chokepoint mistakes surface before design/prep, not at acceptance.
- SC3: Design/review get an auditable record: searched surfaces, found surfaces, excluded surfaces, final scope disposition.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: Every discovery runs an always-on **problem-completeness check**: was the full problem identified, not only an observed symptom/path?
- AC2: Every discovery runs an always-on **solution-scope check**: is the full intended solution scoped, not only one implementation piece?
- AC3: When discovery relies on a sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claim, discovery **blocks** until the claim is verified or downgraded.
- AC4: Any secondary surfaces found during completeness verification must be classified **in scope / out of scope with rationale / unresolved user scope question** before agreement.
- AC5: The obligation is durable in ADV law/docs/tests: `adv-discover` spec + docs mirror + command/checklist surface + asset-test anchor.
- AC6: The completeness **check** always runs; the codebase surface-scan **depth** scales to what the completeness question demands, preserving proportionality for narrow changes.

## Constraints

- C1: Always-on completeness check; no trigger gate. Scan depth scales to the question; narrow changes are not forced into broad repository-wide scans.
- C2: Correctness stays structural: spec law + command/checklist output shape + asset-test anchors own it; heuristics assist only (P33).
- C3: Primary home: `adv-discover` spec/checklist/command. Touch `advance-workflow` only if design finds cross-gate law necessary.
- C4: Coordinate with `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` on any shared `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `advance-workflow` edits.
- C5: Preserve discovery as the phase that firms design-independent objectives, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries.
- C6: Structural checks are tool-layer/spec/checklist/asset anchors only — no new Temporal workflow signal, query, or `defineUpdate` surface.

## Avoidances

- DONT1: Don't treat first-found call sites or first-observed symptoms as proof of full problem/solution scope.
- DONT2: Don't rely on heuristic prose alone where a structural spec/checklist/test anchor can own correctness.
- DONT3: Don't gate the completeness *check* behind a trigger condition — the check is always-on; only scan *depth* scales.
- DONT4: Don't silently defer secondary surfaces as "future work" without explicit scope rationale.
- DONT5: Don't force narrow changes into broad repository-wide scans when the completeness question does not demand it.
- DONT6: Don't replace discovery's P25 related-pattern scan wholesale — extend/specialize it.

## Preview Applicability

`visual_surface: false` — workflow/spec/test change; no front-end, browser-visible, or visual output affected.

## Out of Scope

- OOS1: Editing the pokeedge project or retroactively fixing `addResidentialProxyExternal`.
- OOS2: Designing a language-specific static analyzer for every cross-cutting concern.
- OOS3: Replacing P25 related-pattern scan wholesale.
- OOS4: Changing the seven-gate lifecycle or moving discovery responsibilities into design/review.
- OOS5: Building a target-codebase-aware sole-entry validator (structural enforcement limited to spec + command + checklist + asset anchors).
- OOS6: Adding a new Temporal workflow signal/query/`defineUpdate` surface.

## Decisions

### User Decisions

1. **Trigger scope** — "Don't rely on a trigger; persistent always-on check that the FULL problem was found (not just a symptom) and the FULL solution is scoped (not just a piece)." Why it matters: determines whether every discovery pays a completeness-check cost and whether narrow changes avoid broad scans. Reframed AC1/AC2 (always-on) and AC6 (depth-scaled scan).
2. **Strictness** — "Block until verified." Why it matters: an unverified sole-entry claim cannot silently proceed to design. Adopted as AC3.
3. **Secondary paths** — "Disposition required." Why it matters: found surfaces cannot hide as vague future work. Adopted as AC4.
4. **Title/scope** — Broadened from "Add chokepoint verification" to "Add discovery completeness verification." Chokepoint/sole-entry is one hard-block instance.

### Agent Decisions (LBP)

1. **Primary durable home = `adv-discover` spec** — keeps obligations co-located with `rq-disc08` (P25); locality of behavior. `advance-workflow` touched only if cross-gate law emerges at design.
2. **Extend P25, don't replace** — P25 scans for similar bugs; completeness check verifies full problem/solution scope. Different questions, complementary.
3. **Structural enforcement via spec + command + checklist + asset-test anchors** — pure prose is document-only (current P25 weakness). Strongest durable form without a target-codebase-aware validator.
4. **No external alternatives apply** — internal workflow/spec-law correction; no external library/service/tool is a realistic solution.

## Deferred Questions

- Whether a concise canonical invariant belongs in `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` or whether spec/checklist/command artifacts suffice — deferred to `/adv-design`.

## Sign-Off

User approved criteria via Tier A "approve" after validation pass (F1 proposal-sync, F2 AC6 wording, F3 title broadening all resolved).
documents.design:
# Design — addChokepointVerification (Add Discovery Completeness Verification)

> Change ID immutable; scope broadened at discovery to always-on discovery completeness verification. Chokepoint/sole-entry is one hard-block instance.

## Architecture Overview

Add an **always-on completeness discipline** to the `/adv-discover` command as a new **Phase 1.8: Completeness Verification**, plus matching law in the `adv-discover` spec, a checklist step, a docs mirror, and asset-test anchors. The discipline has two always-on checks and one blocking specialization:

1. **Problem-completeness check (always-on)** — "Was the full problem identified, or only an observed symptom/path?" Discovery records a rationale + confidence.
2. **Solution-scope check (always-on)** — "Is the full intended solution scoped, or only one implementation piece?" Discovery records rationale + confidence + any sole-entry/single-control-surface claims.
3. **Sole-entry blocking specialization** — when discovery relies on a sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claim that is not verified, emit a **B-CRITICAL** ambiguity finding. The existing `rq-disc-tax2` halt-and-handoff-to-`/adv-clarify` path fires — **no new halt machinery**. Secondary surfaces found during verification require explicit disposition (in scope / out of scope with rationale / unresolved user scope question).

Scan depth scales to the question (AC6): the *check* always runs; a broad codebase surface scan (lgrep semantic + symbol) only runs when the completeness question demands it (sole-entry claim or cross-cutting operation). Narrow changes record a lightweight rationale and proceed.

### Relationship to existing mechanisms

- **P25 Related-Pattern Scan (`rq-disc08`, Phase 1.7)**: scans for *similar bugs* after a defect class is known. Completeness verification asks a different question: *did we find the whole problem/solution?* They are **complementary, not merged**.
- **AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS B=Boundaries (`rq-disc-tax1`/`rq-disc-tax2`)**: the sole-entry blocking case reuses this existing CRITICAL→halt→`/adv-clarify` plumbing.
- **`tightenAdvScopeDiscipline`**: prep/review task→AC bounding (downstream of discovery). Discovery-local; no collision.

## Key Decisions

### KD1 — New always-on Phase 1.8, not a trigger gate
Always-on checks (DONT3). The check is lightweight (rationale + confidence); only scan *depth* is conditional (AC6).

### KD2 — Sole-entry blocking reuses B-CRITICAL ambiguity path
Unverified sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claim = `B{N} CRITICAL Boundaries` with verbatim evidence; existing `rq-disc-tax2.1` halts discovery and hands off to `/adv-clarify`. Validator confirmed B=Boundaries is a REQUIRED category (`rq-disc-tax1.1`) and CRITICAL halts + skips gate completion (`rq-disc-tax2.1`). Zero new halt code paths.

### KD3 — Primary spec home: `adv-discover` (not `advance-workflow`)
Locality of behavior (P04). `advance-workflow` **not touched** (C3). Validator confirmed IDs `rq-disc13/14/15` are collision-free (highest existing = `rq-disc12`).

### KD4 — Keep P25 and completeness verification as separate steps
Merging conflates "scan for sibling bugs" with "prove we found the whole problem/solution."

### KD5 — Structural enforcement = spec + command + checklist + asset-test anchors
Validator + scout confirmed `plugin/src/validator/completeness.ts` operates on deltas/tasks, NOT discovery prose — **wrong reuse target**. Asset tests assert *presence* (structural tokens: spec IDs, section headers, phase names), not semantic quality. Quality stays agent-honor with the KD2 blocking backstop.

### KD6 — ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md: pointer-only, no duplicated law
Single-source-per-surface (`adv-instructions-audit` skill).

### KD7 — No new Temporal surface (C6)
No signal/query/`defineUpdate`. Honors `workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts`.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Spec law first** — add `rq-disc13` (problem-completeness), `rq-disc14` (solution-scope + sole-entry blocking + secondary disposition), `rq-disc15` (durable anchors) to `.adv/specs/adv-discover/spec.json`; mirror into `docs/specs/adv-discover.md`. Bump version.
2. **Command contract** — add Phase 1.8 to `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md`; add "Completeness Verification" row to the Required Output Sections table and the Discovery Protocol step list (8→9 steps).
3. **Checklist** — add Phase 1.8 step to `docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md`; "All 10 protocol steps" → 11 at `:24` and `:139`; add edge-case handling row. Canonical insertion point = Output Section Schema table `:60-78`.
4. **Asset anchors** — extend `plugin/src/workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts` (command + checklist anchors); add spec-ID assertions near `adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts:276-295`. **Cross-assert identical protocol step count in both command and checklist** (DDC4 strengthening per validator).
5. **ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md pointer** — one-line in the discovery protocol row.
6. **Verify** — `bin/oc-test targeted`; `pnpm run check`.

Atomic single engineer task (asset tests assert tokens independently → stable commit).

## LBP Analysis

Boring, locally-correct: extend discovery phase with a sibling step to P25; reuse the ambiguity halt path for blocking. The alternative (standalone chokepoint analyzer / target-codebase-aware sole-entry validator) is explicitly OOS (OOS2/OOS5).

## Affected Components

| Component | Change |
|---|---|
| `.adv/specs/adv-discover/spec.json` | +`rq-disc13`, `rq-disc14`, `rq-disc15`; version bump |
| `docs/specs/adv-discover.md` | mirror the three requirements + scenarios |
| `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md` | +Phase 1.8; +output-section row; protocol step count 8→9 |
| `docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md` | +Phase 1.8 step; "10 steps"→11; +edge-case row |
| `plugin/src/workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts` | +anchor assertions (command + checklist) + step-count cross-assert |
| `plugin/src/adv-skill-backed-commands-assets.test.ts` | +spec ID assertions (rq-disc13/14/15) |
| `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` | one-line pointer (no duplicated law) |

## Design-Derived Criteria

Technical criteria caused by the chosen architecture; no new user-facing AC.

- **DDC1 (bounded evidence)** — Completeness-check output must stay bounded: terms-searched ≤ a small N, surfaces listed ≤ top-N with rationale. Prevents always-on check from bloating every discovery output.
- **DDC2 (deterministic anchors)** — Asset-test assertions check *presence* of stable structural tokens (spec IDs, section headers, phase name "Completeness Verification"), not prose quality. Keeps CI deterministic.
- **DDC3 (no new Temporal surface)** — C6 honored; enforcement is command/spec/checklist/test only.
- **DDC4 (step-count invariant, strengthened)** — The asset test **cross-asserts the identical protocol step count** in both `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md` and `docs/checklists/discover-checklist.md`, so the command (9 steps) and checklist (11 protocol items) cannot drift independently. Converts the weakest anchor from presence-only to drift-resistant (validator recommendation).

## Risks / Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| R1: Always-on check adds friction to every discovery | Check is lightweight (rationale + confidence); deep scan only on sole-entry claim / cross-cutting op (AC6). |
| R2: Asset-test brittleness on prose | Anchor on stable tokens (spec IDs, headers), not sentences (DDC2). |
| R3: Document-only enforcement weakness persists (depth/quality of the check is agent-honor) | Acceptable per OOS5; KD2 provides the blocking backstop for the highest-stakes case. |
| R4: Coordination with `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` | Discovery-local; sibling is prep/review. Only shared surface is `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`. |
| R5: lgrep semantic search timeouts | Existing fallback to symbol/text search; completeness scan inherits the degradation path. |

## Validation

### Design Leverage Scout
5 candidates, all auto-adopt (low risk, contract-tied, no user-value tradeoff):
1. `completeness.ts` is the WRONG reuse target → confirms KD5.
2. Sole-entry block reuses `rq-disc-tax2` B-CRITICAL halt → confirms KD2.
3. "All 10 protocol steps" lockstep drift → 11 → captured as DDC4.
4. Output Section Schema table (`:60-78`) is the canonical single insertion point → captured in implementation strategy step 3.
5. Single atomic engineer task viable → captured in implementation strategy.

### Independent Design Validator
**Verdict: VALIDATED** (one CAUTION, no CONFLICT, no contract compromise).

- Correctness: three-part discipline maps cleanly to all six ACs; blocking case verified against `rq-disc-tax1.1` / `rq-disc-tax2.1`.
- Simplicity: boring, locally-correct; no materially simpler approach meets AC3+AC5 together.
- Spec-law compliance: no contradiction; `rq-disc13/14/15` collision-free; `advance-workflow` untouched per C3.
- Key alternatives: completeness.ts reuse correctly rejected. CAUTION → DDC4 step-count cross-assert (integrated above).

**Prep action from validator:** asset test cross-asserts identical protocol step count in command and checklist (DDC4) — converts weakest structural anchor from presence-only to drift-resistant. Integrated into DDC4 and implementation strategy step 4.

Coordinator: no user-value tradeoff, no unresolved CONFLICT, no contract-compromise risk, validator VALIDATED → proceed to `/adv-prep` after gate completion.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — Add Discovery Completeness Verification

`/adv-discover` now runs an always-on **Completeness Verification** step (Phase 1.8) that prevents discovery from treating a found symptom or single code path as the full problem/full solution. The motivating failure — `addResidentialProxyExternal` missing a parallel `ImageDownloadClient` download surface — is now structurally guarded: discovery must answer whether the full problem was identified and the full solution is scoped, every time.

**What shipped:**
- Three new spec requirements in `adv-discover` (v1.4.0): `rq-disc13` (always-on problem-completeness check), `rq-disc14` (solution-scope check + sole-entry blocking + secondary-surface disposition), `rq-disc15` (durable anchors across spec/command/checklist/docs/tests).
- New Phase 1.8 in `.opencode/command/adv-discover.md`: two always-on checks, scan-depth scaling (narrow changes record a lightweight rationale; broad scans run only for sole-entry/cross-cutting claims), sole-entry blocking that **reuses the existing B-CRITICAL ambiguity halt** (`rq-disc-tax2` → `/adv-clarify`) rather than new machinery, and mandatory secondary-surface disposition before agreement.
- `discover-checklist.md`, `docs/specs/adv-discover.md` mirror, and a one-line `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` pointer kept in sync.
- Asset tests assert the obligation is co-present across spec, command, checklist, and docs mirror (rq-disc15 drift guard).

**Key design decision:** the sole-entry block deliberately reuses the existing Boundaries-CRITICAL → halt → `/adv-clarify` plumbing (KD2). No new halt code, no new Temporal surface (C6). `advance-workflow` was left untouched (C3); only a stale test assertion (1.17.0→1.18.0) was campsite-fixed in the touched test file.

**Design refinement during execution:** `rq-disc15.2` was revised from "protocol step count equality" to "co-presence" — the command (9 steps) and checklist (11 items) use different granularities, so numeric equality would be false; co-presence is the real drift guarantee. Recorded in spec + docs mirror.

**Verified:** RED→GREEN TDD per implementation task; 67/67 asset tests pass; `pnpm run check` exit 0 (schemas/typecheck/lint/format); independent reviewer verdict READY (one in-scope AC5 gap remediated); contract review matrix 27/27 pass, 0 failing. No scope drift.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-19T04:36:00.000Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | Discovery stops treating "found one symptom/path" as "found the full problem / full solution" without explicit completeness evidence. | pass | Phase 1.8 problem-completeness check (adv-discover.md) + rq-disc13 require explicit rationale before a symptom is treated as the full problem. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | Sole-path / chokepoint mistakes surface before design/prep, not at acceptance. | pass | rq-disc14.2 + Phase 1.8 sole-entry blocking (B-CRITICAL via rq-disc-tax2) surfaces unverified chokepoint claims before design/prep. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Design/review get an auditable record: searched surfaces, found surfaces, excluded surfaces, final scope disposition. | pass | Phase 1.8 target-operation surface scan evidence shape (searched terms/symbols, found/excluded surfaces, disposition) gives design/review an auditable record. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | Every discovery runs an always-on **problem-completeness check**: was the full problem identified, not only an observed symptom/path? | pass | workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts command contains 'problem-completeness'; Phase 1.8 step in adv-discover.md. 67/67 tests pass. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | Every discovery runs an always-on **solution-scope check**: is the full intended solution scoped, not only one implementation piece? | pass | workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts command contains 'solution-scope'; Phase 1.8 solution-scope check present. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | When discovery relies on a sole-chokepoint / single-entry / single-control-surface claim, discovery **blocks** until the claim is verified or downgraded. | pass | workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts B-CRITICAL/Boundaries CRITICAL; rq-disc14.2 + Phase 1.8 sole-entry blocking reuse rq-disc-tax2 halt. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Any secondary surfaces found during completeness verification must be classified **in scope / out of scope with rationale / unresolved user scope question** before agreement. | pass | Phase 1.8 secondary-surface disposition (in/out/unsolved) in command + checklist edge-case rows; discover-checklist.md requires classification before agreement. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | The obligation is durable in ADV law/docs/tests: `adv-discover` spec + docs mirror + command/checklist surface + asset-test anchor. | pass | rq-disc13/14/15 in spec.json + docs mirror; Phase 1.8 in command + checklist; ADV_INSTRUCTIONS pointer; asset anchors in 2 test files. workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts spec IDs + command + checklist + docs mirror co-presence. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | The completeness **check** always runs; the codebase surface-scan **depth** scales to what the completeness question demands, preserving proportionality for narrow changes. | pass | workflow-noise-reduction-assets.test.ts asserts 'scan depth scales'; Phase 1.8 scan-depth-scaling section + rq-disc13.2. |
| C1 | constraint | Always-on completeness check; no trigger gate. Scan depth scales to the question; narrow changes are not forced into broad repository-wide scans. | respected | Phase 1.8 is always-on (not trigger-gated); scan-depth-scaling section in adv-discover.md + checklist edge row. |
| C2 | constraint | Correctness stays structural: spec law + command/checklist output shape + asset-test anchors own it; heuristics assist only (P33). | respected | Spec law (rq-disc13/14/15) + command + checklist + asset-test anchors own the obligation; no heuristic-only prose. |
| C3 | constraint | Primary home: `adv-discover` spec/checklist/command. Touch `advance-workflow` only if design finds cross-gate law necessary. | respected | Primary home adv-discover; advance-workflow spec untouched (only stale test assertion campsite-fixed 1.17→1.18). |
| C4 | constraint | Coordinate with `tightenAdvScopeDiscipline` on any shared `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` / `advance-workflow` edits. | respected | ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md edit is a one-line pointer only (single-source-per-surface); sibling change touches prep/review. |
| C5 | constraint | Preserve discovery as the phase that firms design-independent objectives, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries. | respected | Discovery still firms design-independent objectives/AC; completeness check does not move criteria firming out of discovery. |
| C6 | constraint | Structural checks are tool-layer/spec/checklist/asset anchors only — no new Temporal workflow signal, query, or `defineUpdate` surface. | respected | No new Temporal signal/query/defineUpdate; enforcement is spec/command/checklist/test only. workflow-bundle-boundary untouched. |
| DONT1 | avoidance | Don't treat first-found call sites or first-observed symptoms as proof of full problem/solution scope. | respected | Phase 1.8 explicitly requires verifying sole-entry claims; rq-disc14.2 blocks unverified claims. |
| DONT2 | avoidance | Don't rely on heuristic prose alone where a structural spec/checklist/test anchor can own correctness. | respected | Obligation owned by spec rq-* + asset tests, not prose alone. |
| DONT3 | avoidance | Don't gate the completeness *check* behind a trigger condition — the check is always-on; only scan *depth* scales. | respected | Phase 1.8 runs always-on; only scan depth is conditional. |
| DONT4 | avoidance | Don't silently defer secondary surfaces as "future work" without explicit scope rationale. | respected | Phase 1.8 + checklist forbid silently deferring secondary surfaces; require explicit disposition. |
| DONT5 | avoidance | Don't force narrow changes into broad repository-wide scans when the completeness question does not demand it. | respected | Scan-depth-scaling section + rq-disc13.2 record lightweight rationale for narrow changes; no forced broad scan. |
| DONT6 | avoidance | Don't replace discovery's P25 related-pattern scan wholesale — extend/specialize it. | respected | Phase 1.8 is separate from P25 (rq-disc08); documented as complementary, not merged. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Editing the pokeedge project or retroactively fixing `addResidentialProxyExternal`. | not_applicable | pokeedge not edited; only ADV repo surfaces touched. |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | Designing a language-specific static analyzer for every cross-cutting concern. | not_applicable | No language-specific static analyzer built. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | Replacing P25 related-pattern scan wholesale. | not_applicable | P25 (rq-disc08) preserved; Phase 1.8 is additive. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Changing the seven-gate lifecycle or moving discovery responsibilities into design/review. | not_applicable | Seven-gate lifecycle unchanged; discovery still owns agreement. |
| OOS5 | out_of_scope | Building a target-codebase-aware sole-entry validator (structural enforcement limited to spec + command + checklist + asset anchors). | not_applicable | No target-codebase-aware validator; enforcement is spec/command/checklist/test anchors only. |
| OOS6 | out_of_scope | Adding a new Temporal workflow signal/query/`defineUpdate` surface. | not_applicable | No new Temporal surface added. |
